typeData.behavior:
; 7408 — quad 2-input AND gate. Derived from the function table
; (datasheet Table 3): each output nY is HIGH only when both of that
; gate's inputs are HIGH, and LOW otherwise.
;
; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,
; so the behavior is one sum-of-products equation per unit, each using that
; unit's own pin names. (renderas: and fixes the drawn symbol; these
; equations are the authoritative logic.)
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (no input here is active-low). Each output is a single AND term
; at the output's active-high polarity: nY = nA * nB.
1Y = 1A * 1B
2Y = 2A * 2B
3Y = 3A * 3B
4Y = 4A * 4B

; 7432 — quad 2-input OR gate. Derived from the function table (datasheet
; Table 3): each output nY is HIGH when either of that gate's inputs is
; HIGH, and LOW only when both inputs are LOW.
;
; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,
; so the behavior is one sum-of-products equation per unit, each using that
; unit's own pin names. (renderas: or fixes the drawn symbol; these
; equations are the authoritative logic.)
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (no input here is active-low). The OR output is active-high and
; needs no complement: nY = nA + nB.
1Y = 1A + 1B
2Y = 2A + 2B
3Y = 3A + 3B
4Y = 4A + 4B

;
; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,

1Y = 1A + 1B
2Y = 2A + 2B
3Y = 3A + 3B
4Y = 4A + 4B

; 7486 — quad 2-input EXCLUSIVE-OR gate. Derived from the function table
; (datasheet Table 3): each output nY is HIGH when its two inputs differ and
; LOW when they are equal.
;
; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,
; so the behavior is one sum-of-products equation per unit, each using that
; unit's own pin names. (renderas: xor fixes the drawn symbol; these
; equations are the authoritative logic.)
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (no input here is active-low). The XOR output is active-high and
; written as the two differing-input product terms: nY = nA*/nB + /nA*nB.
1Y = 1A * /1B + /1A * 1B
2Y = 2A * /2B + /2A * 2B
3Y = 3A * /3B + /3A * 3B
4Y = 4A * /4B + /4A * 4B

; 7486 — quad 2-input EXCLUSIVE-OR gate. Derived from the function table

; LOW when they are equal.

; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,

; dropped (no input here is active-low). The XOR output is active-high and
; written as the two differing-input product terms: nY = nA*/nB + /nA*nB.
1Y = 1A * /1B + /1A * 1B
2Y = 2A * /2B + /2A * 2B
3Y = 3A * /3B + /3A * 3B
4Y = 4A * /4B + /4A * 4B

; 74283 — 4-bit binary full adder, S = A + B + CIN (A0/B0/S0 = LSB).
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and a
; signal name is this file's pin name (the adder pins are all active-high, so
; no '/' prefixes appear). ':+:' is the extended-dialect XOR operator
; (FR-079a) — without it, a flat sum-of-products sum bit explodes (the MSB to
; ~256 terms), so XOR is what makes this part expressible.
;
; Each sum bit is Sn = An XOR Bn XOR Cn, where Cn is the carry into bit n:
;   C0 = CIN, and C(n+1) = An*Bn + (An + Bn)*Cn  (generate + propagate*carry).
; The carries Cn are not package pins, so each is inlined as the flat
; sum-of-products of the primary inputs (the GAL has no internal nodes and no
; parentheses). COUT is the bit-4 carry (the part's fast-carry output). The
; equations were machine-generated and verified against arithmetic for all
; 512 input combinations.
S0 = A0 :+: B0 :+: CIN
S1 = A1 :+: B1 :+: A0 * B0 + A0 * CIN + B0 * CIN
S2 = A2 :+: B2 :+: A1 * B1 + A0 * B0 * A1 + A0 * A1 * CIN + B0 * A1 * CIN + A0 * B0 * B1 + A0 * B1 * CIN + B0 * B1 * CIN
S3 = A3 :+: B3 :+: A2 * B2 + A1 * B1 * A2 + A0 * B0 * A1 * A2 + A0 * A1 * A2 * CIN + B0 * A1 * A2 * CIN + A0 * B0 * B1 * A2 + A0 * B1 * A2 * CIN + B0 * B1 * A2 * CIN + A1 * B1 * B2 + A0 * B0 * A1 * B2 + A0 * A1 * B2 * CIN + B0 * A1 * B2 * CIN + A0 * B0 * B1 * B2 + A0 * B1 * B2 * CIN + B0 * B1 * B2 * CIN
COUT = A3 * B3 + A2 * B2 * A3 + A1 * B1 * A2 * A3 + A0 * B0 * A1 * A2 * A3 + A0 * A1 * A2 * A3 * CIN + B0 * A1 * A2 * A3 * CIN + A0 * B0 * B1 * A2 * A3 + A0 * B1 * A2 * A3 * CIN + B0 * B1 * A2 * A3 * CIN + A1 * B1 * B2 * A3 + A0 * B0 * A1 * B2 * A3 + A0 * A1 * B2 * A3 * CIN + B0 * A1 * B2 * A3 * CIN + A0 * B0 * B1 * B2 * A3 + A0 * B1 * B2 * A3 * CIN + B0 * B1 * B2 * A3 * CIN + A2 * B2 * B3 + A1 * B1 * A2 * B3 + A0 * B0 * A1 * A2 * B3 + A0 * A1 * A2 * B3 * CIN + B0 * A1 * A2 * B3 * CIN + A0 * B0 * B1 * A2 * B3 + A0 * B1 * A2 * B3 * CIN + B0 * B1 * A2 * B3 * CIN + A1 * B1 * B2 * B3 + A0 * B0 * A1 * B2 * B3 + A0 * A1 * B2 * B3 * CIN + B0 * A1 * B2 * B3 * CIN + A0 * B0 * B1 * B2 * B3 + A0 * B1 * B2 * B3 * CIN + B0 * B1 * B2 * B3 * CIN
typeData.description: 4-bit binary full adder with fast carry

datasheet.title: CD74HC283 High-Speed CMOS Logic 4-Bit Binary Full Adder with Fast Carry

typeData.name: pulldown

typeData.icon: <svg width="36" height="36" viewBox="0 0 36 36" aria-hidden="true" fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><line x1="18" y1="4" x2="18" y2="28"/><line x1="8" y1="28" x2="28" y2="28"/></svg>

typeData.behavior:
; 74244 — octal non-inverting 3-state buffer/line driver in two 4-bit
; groups, each group gated by its own active-low output enable. Derived
; from the function table (datasheet Table 3): with /nOE LOW each output
; nYn follows data input nAn; with /nOE HIGH that group's outputs are in
; the high-impedance OFF-state.
;
; 3-state outputs use GALasm's tristate construct (galasmManual.txt section
; 3.4, established on the 74245): each output has a .T equation fed from its
; data input plus a single-term .E enable. The pin drives only while its .E
; term is true; when /nOE is HIGH the .E term is false and the output is
; high-impedance.
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (pin /1OE is GAL signal 1OE, so '/1OE' below means "pin /1OE is
; LOW").
1Y0.T = 1A0
1Y0.E = /1OE
1Y1.T = 1A1
1Y1.E = /1OE
1Y2.T = 1A2
1Y2.E = /1OE
1Y3.T = 1A3
1Y3.E = /1OE
2Y0.T = 2A0
2Y0.E = /2OE
2Y1.T = 2A1
2Y1.E = /2OE
2Y2.T = 2A2
2Y2.E = /2OE
2Y3.T = 2A3
2Y3.E = /2OE

; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (pin /1OE is GAL signal 1OE, so '/1OE' below means "pin /1OE is
; LOW").
1Y0.T = 1A0
1Y0.E = /1OE
1Y1.T = 1A1
1Y1.E = /1OE
1Y2.T = 1A2
1Y2.E = /1OE
1Y3.T = 1A3
1Y3.E = /1OE
2Y0.T = 2A0
2Y0.E = /2OE
2Y1.T = 2A1
2Y1.E = /2OE
2Y2.T = 2A2
2Y2.E = /2OE
2Y3.T = 2A3
2Y3.E = /2OE

typeData.behavior:
; 74244 — octal non-inverting 3-state buffer/line driver in two 4-bit
; groups, each group gated by its own active-low output enable. Derived
; from the function table (datasheet Table 3): with /nOE LOW each output
; nYn follows data input nAn; with /nOE HIGH that group's outputs are in
; the high-impedance OFF-state.
;
; 3-state outputs use GALasm's tristate construct (galasmManual.txt section
; 3.4, established on the 74245): each output has a .T equation fed from its
; data input plus a single-term .E enable. The pin drives only while its .E
; term is true; when /nOE is HIGH the .E term is false and the output is
; high-impedance.
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (pin /1OE is GAL signal 1OE, so '/1OE' below means "pin /1OE is
; LOW").
1Y0.T = 1A0
1Y0.E = /1OE
1Y1.T = 1A1
1Y1.E = /1OE
1Y2.T = 1A2
1Y2.E = /1OE
1Y3.T = 1A3
1Y3.E = /1OE
2Y0.T = 2A0
2Y0.E = /2OE
2Y1.T = 2A1
2Y1.E = /2OE
2Y2.T = 2A2
2Y2.E = /2OE
2Y3.T = 2A3
2Y3.E = /2OE

typeData.behavior:
; 74138 — 3-to-8 line decoder/demultiplexer, inverting.
; Derived from the function table (datasheet Table 3): every output is HIGH
; unless E1 and E2 are LOW and E3 is HIGH; when enabled, the one output
; addressed by A2..A0 (A0 = LSB) is LOW.
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (pin /E1 is GAL signal E1, so '/E1' below means "pin /E1 is
; LOW"). Each output is a single AND term; '/Yn = term' drives pin Yn LOW
; exactly when the term is true, so the three disabled rows of the function
; table (all outputs HIGH) fall out of the same equations.
/Y0 = /E1 * /E2 * E3 * /A2 * /A1 * /A0
/Y1 = /E1 * /E2 * E3 * /A2 * /A1 *  A0
/Y2 = /E1 * /E2 * E3 * /A2 *  A1 * /A0
/Y3 = /E1 * /E2 * E3 * /A2 *  A1 *  A0
/Y4 = /E1 * /E2 * E3 *  A2 * /A1 * /A0
/Y5 = /E1 * /E2 * E3 *  A2 * /A1 *  A0
/Y6 = /E1 * /E2 * E3 *  A2 *  A1 * /A0
/Y7 = /E1 * /E2 * E3 *  A2 *  A1 *  A0

typeData.behavior:
; 7400 — quad 2-input NAND gate. Derived from the function table
; (datasheet Table 3): each output nY is LOW only when both of that gate's
; inputs are HIGH, and HIGH otherwise.
;
; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,
; so the behavior is one sum-of-products equation per unit, each using that
; unit's own pin names. (renderas: nand fixes the drawn symbol; these
; equations are the authoritative logic.)
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (no input here is active-low). Outputs are active-high, so NAND
; is written at the output's declared polarity using De Morgan:
; nY = /nA + /nB ("nY is HIGH when nA is LOW or nB is LOW").
1Y = /1A + /1B
2Y = /2A + /2B
3Y = /3A + /3B
4Y = /4A + /4B

; 7400 — quad 2-input NAND gate. Derived from the function table
; (datasheet Table 3): each output nY is LOW only when both of that gate's
; inputs are HIGH, and HIGH otherwise.
;
; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,
; so the behavior is one sum-of-products equation per unit, each using that
; unit's own pin names. (renderas: nand fixes the drawn symbol; these
; equations are the authoritative logic.)
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (no input here is active-low). Outputs are active-high, so NAND
; is written at the output's declared polarity using De Morgan:
; nY = /nA + /nB ("nY is HIGH when nA is LOW or nB is LOW").
1Y = /1A + /1B
2Y = /2A + /2B
3Y = /3A + /3B
4Y = /4A + /4B

; 7408 — quad 2-input AND gate. Derived from the function table
; (datasheet Table 3): each output nY is HIGH only when both of that
; gate's inputs are HIGH, and LOW otherwise.
;
; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,

2Y = 2A * 2B
3Y = 3A * 3B
4Y = 4A * 4B
